title: Perfectionism Flexibility Maintenance Checklist
description: A regular check-in tool for maintaining flexibility and preventing perfectionism relapse. Use this checklist weekly or monthly to monitor your patterns and keep your skills active.
instructions: Review each item and check off what applies to your experience over the past week. This is not a test — it is a self-monitoring tool to help you stay aware of your patterns.

items.label: I reviewed my personal early warning signs for perfectionism relapse
I checked whether my standards have been creeping upward in any area
I noticed and managed any checking or correcting behaviors
I deliberately completed at least one task at 'good enough' rather than perfect
I allowed something to be imperfect without trying to fix it
I delegated a task or accepted help without micromanaging
I took time to rest, relax, or do something enjoyable without guilt
I used a thought record or cognitive restructuring when I noticed a rigid rule
I practiced self-compassion when I fell short of a standard
I made a choice based on my values rather than my perfectionistic rules
I evaluated whether my effort in any area was producing diminishing returns
I set a limit or said no to protect my energy and well-being
I acknowledged my progress in managing perfectionism, even small steps

disclaimer: This checklist is for educational self-monitoring. It is not a diagnostic tool or a substitute for professional mental health care. If perfectionism is significantly affecting your daily life, please consider speaking with a qualified mental health professional.
